posts.caption:
Winter is hard on cars. Most people don't realize how hard until something breaks in April.

Road salt corrodes brake lines, exhaust components, and suspension hardware. Potholes knock alignments out and damage struts. Temperature swings stress batteries, belts, and hoses. Moisture gets into brake fluid and reduces stopping power.

None of this shows up on a dashboard light. It shows up on a lift.

That's why a post-winter inspection matters. Not a quick glance. A real inspection — underneath the vehicle, where the damage hides.

We check what most shops skip:
→ Brake lines and hoses for corrosion
→ Suspension components for play and damage
→ Exhaust for rust-through
→ Undercarriage for salt damage
→ Fluids for contamination
→ Belts and hoses for cracking

If everything checks out, you drive away with peace of mind. If something needs attention, you catch it now — before it leaves you on the side of I-90 in April.

📍 Nick's Tire & Auto — 17625 Euclid Ave, Cleveland
📞 (216) 862-0005

Spring is coming. Make sure your car is ready for it.

#PostWinterInspection #ClevelandAuto #NicksTireAndAuto #SpringCarCare #ClevelandMechanic #AutoRepair #BrakeInspection #SuspensionCheck #ClevelandOhio #NortheastOhio #CarMaintenance #TireShopCleveland #MechanicLife #HonestMechanic #AutoService

ALERT 🚨 Cleveland temps are dropping fast.

Here's what that means for your tires:

For every 10°F drop in temperature, your tires lose about 1 PSI of pressure. That means the tires that were fine last week could be 3-4 PSI low right now.

Low tire pressure means:
→ Less grip on wet and icy roads
→ Longer stopping distances
→ Uneven tread wear that kills your tires early
→ Worse fuel economy

Most people don't check until the TPMS light comes on. By then, you've already been driving on underinflated tires for days.

It takes 2 minutes. Check your pressure. Your door jamb sticker tells you the correct PSI.

If your TPMS light is already on — or if you're not sure what your pressure should be — stop by. We'll check it for free.

📍 Nick's Tire & Auto — 17625 Euclid Ave, Cleveland
📞 (216) 862-0005

Save this post. Share it with someone who drives in this weather.

Your engine has a heartbeat.

That dark, sludgy oil on the dipstick? That's the sound of it struggling.

Over time, motor oil breaks down completely. It stops lubricating and starts damaging the very parts it's supposed to protect. That's when a $60 oil change turns into a $4,000 engine repair.

We drain the old. We replace the filter. We fill it with the exact full-synthetic oil your engine needs to perform at its peak.

Keeping Cleveland's engines running smoothly. That's what we do.

📍 Nick's Tire & Auto — Cleveland, OH
📞 Call or text to schedule your oil change today.

One pothole. Four problems. 🚧

Cleveland roads aren’t kind. One hard hit can cause bent rims, knocked-out alignment, sidewall bubbles, and damaged struts. 

If your car feels “off” or pull to one side, don’t ignore it. It’s physics dragging your tires to an early grave. 

Stop by for a suspension check. We’ll get you straightened out.

That squeal isn’t normal. It’s a cry for help. 🛑

Brake noise is your car’s early warning system. Ignore it, and you’re looking at metal-on-metal contact, warped rotors, and a much bigger bill. 

We specialize in high-performance braking systems that stop when you need them to. No guessing, no squealing, just safety. 

Same-day brake service available.

Your tires are lying to you. 📉

They might look fine from a distance, but the truth is in the tread. 

Uneven wear, sidewall cracks, and low tread depth aren’t just maintenance items — they’re safety risks. If your steering wheel is vibrating at speed, your car is trying to tell you something. 

Don’t wait for a blowout. 

Get a FREE tire inspection today at Nick’s.

The biggest mistake we see? Replacing a battery that was actually fine. 📉
Your charging system is a team—if the alternator or the wiring isn’t doing its job, a brand-new battery won’t save you. We’re Nick’s Tire & Auto, and we specialize in the electrical “brain” of your vehicle.
